notes: commit=913308263bdc8042af74924b68dc39a374ad071d; extraction_method=regex_best_effort; doc_density_composite=100.0; params are name-only placeholders (annotation='Any') -- EXP-1 classifier tests selection only, not call-correctness.

tools.name: ${tool.name}
tools.docstring: ${tool.description}

tools.name: uid
tools.docstring: The uid of an element on the page from the page content snapshot

tools.name: dblClick
tools.docstring: Set to true for double clicks. Default is false.

tools.name: includeSnapshot
tools.docstring: Whether to include a snapshot in the response. Default is false.

tools.name: x
tools.docstring: The x coordinate

tools.name: y
tools.docstring: The y coordinate

tools.name: filePath
tools.docstring: A path to the .heapsnapshot file to close.

tools.name: pageId
tools.docstring: The ID of the page to close. Call list_pages to list pages.

tools.name: baseFilePath
tools.docstring: A path to the base .heapsnapshot file (earlier snapshot).

tools.name: currentFilePath
tools.docstring: A path to the current .heapsnapshot file (later snapshot).

tools.name: classIndex
tools.docstring: Optional 0-based index of the class in the summary list to filter results, showing individual objects.

tools.name: from_uid
tools.docstring: The uid of the element to drag

tools.name: to_uid
tools.docstring: The uid of the element to drop into

tools.name: networkConditions
tools.docstring: Throttle network. Omit to disable throttling.

tools.name: cpuThrottlingRate
tools.docstring: Represents the CPU slowdown factor. Omit or set the rate to 1 to disable throttling

tools.name: geolocation
tools.docstring: Geolocation (`<latitude>,<longitude>`) to emulate. Latitude between -90 and 90. Longitude between -180 and 180. Omit to clear the geolocation override.

tools.name: userAgent
tools.docstring: User agent to emulate. Set to empty string to clear the user agent override.

tools.name: colorScheme
tools.docstring: Emulate the dark or the light mode. Set to "auto" to reset to the default.

tools.name: viewport
tools.docstring: Emulate device viewports '<width>x<height>x<devicePixelRatio>[,mobile][,touch][,landscape]'. 'touch' and 'mobile' to emulate mobile devices. 'landscape' to emulate landscape mode.

tools.name: extraHttpHeaders
tools.docstring: Extra HTTP headers as a JSON string object, e.g. {"X-Custom": "value", "Authorization": "Bearer token"}. Headers are included into every HTTP request originating from the page and persist across navigations until cleared. Pass an empty string to clear all extra headers.

tools.name: function
tools.docstring: A JavaScript function declaration to be executed by the tool in the currently selected page. Example without arguments: `() => document.title` or `async () => await fetch("example.com")`. Example with arguments: `(el) => el.innerText`

tools.name: args
tools.docstring: An optional list of arguments to pass to the function.

tools.name: dialogAction
tools.docstring: Handle dialogs while execution. "accept", "dismiss", or string for response of window.prompt. Defaults to accept.

tools.name: toolName
tools.docstring: The name of the tool to execute

tools.name: params
tools.docstring: The JSON-stringified parameters to pass to the tool

tools.name: input
tools.docstring: The JSON-stringified parameters to pass to the WebMCP tool

tools.name: value
tools.docstring: The value to fill in. "true" or "false" for checkboxes and toggles, "true" for radio buttons.

tools.name: msgid
tools.docstring: The msgid of a console message on the page from the listed console messages

tools.name: id
tools.docstring: The ID for the class, obtained from details.

tools.name: pageIdx
tools.docstring: The page index for pagination.

tools.name: pageSize
tools.docstring: The page size for pagination.

tools.name: nodeId
tools.docstring: The node ID to get the dominator chain for.

tools.name: maxDepth
tools.docstring: The maximum depth to search for retaining paths.

tools.name: maxNodes
tools.docstring: The maximum number of nodes to return.

tools.name: maxSiblings
tools.docstring: The maximum number of siblings to return.

tools.name: reqid
tools.docstring: The reqid of the network request. If omitted returns the currently selected request in the DevTools Network panel.

tools.name: requestFilePath
tools.docstring: The absolute or relative path to a .network-request file to save the request body to. If omitted, the body is returned inline.

tools.name: responseFilePath
tools.docstring: The absolute or relative path to a .network-response file to save the response body to. If omitted, the body is returned inline.

tools.name: action
tools.docstring: Whether to dismiss or accept the dialog

tools.name: promptText
tools.docstring: Optional prompt text to enter into the dialog.

tools.name: path
tools.docstring: Absolute path to the unpacked extension folder.

tools.name: mode
tools.docstring: "navigation" reloads & audits. "snapshot" analyzes current state.

tools.name: device
tools.docstring: Device to emulate.

tools.name: outputDirPath
tools.docstring: Directory for reports. If omitted, uses temporary files.

tools.name: types
tools.docstring: Filter messages to only return messages of the specified resource types. When omitted or empty, returns all messages.

tools.name: includePreservedMessages
tools.docstring: Set to true to return the preserved messages over the last 3 navigations.

tools.name: serviceWorkerId
tools.docstring: Filter messages to only return messages of the specified service worker.

tools.name: resourceTypes
tools.docstring: Filter requests to only return requests of the specified resource types. When omitted or empty, returns all requests.

tools.name: includePreservedRequests
tools.docstring: Set to true to return the preserved requests over the last 3 navigations.

tools.name: type
tools.docstring: Navigate the page by URL, back or forward in history, or reload.

tools.name: url
tools.docstring: Target URL (only type=url)

tools.name: ignoreCache
tools.docstring: Whether to ignore cache on reload.

tools.name: handleBeforeUnload
tools.docstring: Whether to auto accept or beforeunload dialogs triggered by this navigation. Default is accept.

tools.name: initScript
tools.docstring: A JavaScript script to be executed on each new document before any other scripts for the next navigation.

tools.name: timeout
tools.docstring: Maximum wait time in milliseconds. If set to 0, the default timeout will be used.

tools.name: background
tools.docstring: Whether to open the page in the background without bringing it to the front. Default is false (foreground).

tools.name: isolatedContext
tools.docstring: If specified, the page is created in an isolated browser context with the given name. Pages in the same browser context share cookies and storage. Pages in different browser contexts are fully isolated.

tools.name: insightSetId
tools.docstring: The id for the specific insight set. Only use the ids given in the "Available insight sets" list.

tools.name: insightName
tools.docstring: The name of the Insight you want more information on. For example: "DocumentLatency" or "LCPBreakdown"

tools.name: reload
tools.docstring: Determines if, once tracing has started, the current selected page should be automatically reloaded. Navigate the page to the right URL using the navigate_page tool BEFORE starting the trace if reload or autoStop is set to true.

tools.name: autoStop
tools.docstring: Determines if the trace recording should be automatically stopped.

tools.name: key
tools.docstring: A key or a combination (e.g., "Enter", "Control+A", "Control++", "Control+Shift+R"). Modifiers: Control, Shift, Alt, Meta

tools.name: width
tools.docstring: Page width

tools.name: height
tools.docstring: Page height

tools.name: bringToFront
tools.docstring: Whether to focus the page and bring it to the top.

tools.name: format
tools.docstring: Type of format to save the screenshot as. Default is "png"

tools.name: quality
tools.docstring: Compression quality for JPEG and WebP formats (0-100). Higher values mean better quality but larger file sizes. Ignored for PNG format.

tools.name: fullPage
tools.docstring: If set to true takes a screenshot of the full page instead of the currently visible viewport. Incompatible with uid.

tools.name: verbose
tools.docstring: Whether to include all possible information available in the full a11y tree. Default is false.

tools.name: text
tools.docstring: The text to type

tools.name: submitKey
tools.docstring: Optional key to press after typing. E.g., "Enter", "Tab", "Escape"

tools.name: get_console_message
tools.docstring: Gets a console message by its ID. You can get all messages by calling ${LIST_CONSOLE_MESSAGES_TOOL_NAME}.

tools.name: emulate
tools.docstring: Emulates various features on the selected page.

tools.name: install_extension
tools.docstring: Installs a Chrome extension from the given path.

tools.name: uninstall_extension
tools.docstring: Uninstalls a Chrome extension by its ID.

tools.name: list_extensions
tools.docstring: Lists all the Chrome extensions installed in the browser. This includes their name, ID, version, and enabled status.

tools.name: reload_extension
tools.docstring: Reloads an unpacked Chrome extension by its ID.

tools.name: trigger_extension_action
tools.docstring: Triggers the default action of an extension by its ID.

tools.name: click
tools.docstring: Clicks on the provided element

tools.name: click_at
tools.docstring: Clicks at the provided coordinates

tools.name: hover
tools.docstring: Hover over the provided element

tools.name: fill
tools.docstring: Type text into an input, text area or select an option from a <select> element.

tools.name: type_text
tools.docstring: Type text using keyboard into a previously focused input

tools.name: drag
tools.docstring: Drag an element onto another element

tools.name: fill_form
tools.docstring: Fill out multiple form elements (inputs, selects, checkboxes, radios) at once. ALWAYS prefer this tool over multiple individual 'fill' or 'click' calls when interacting with forms. It is significantly faster, more reliable, and reduces turn count. Example: Fill username, password, and check "Remember Me" in one call.

tools.name: upload_file
tools.docstring: Upload a file through a provided element.

tools.name: press_key
tools.docstring: Press a key or key combination. Use this when other input methods like fill() cannot be used (e.g., keyboard shortcuts, navigation keys, or special key combinations).

tools.name: lighthouse_audit
tools.docstring: Get Lighthouse score and reports for accessibility, SEO, best practices, and agentic browsing. This excludes performance. For performance audits, run ${startTrace.name}

tools.name: take_heapsnapshot
tools.docstring: Capture a heap snapshot of the currently selected page. Use to analyze the memory distribution of JavaScript objects and debug memory leaks.

tools.name: get_heapsnapshot_summary
tools.docstring: Loads a memory heapsnapshot and returns snapshot summary stats.

tools.name: get_heapsnapshot_details
tools.docstring: Loads a memory heapsnapshot and returns all available information including statistics, static data, and aggregated node information. Supports pagination for aggregates.

tools.name: get_heapsnapshot_class_nodes
tools.docstring: Loads a memory heapsnapshot and returns instances of a specific class with their IDs.

tools.name: get_heapsnapshot_retainers
tools.docstring: Loads a memory heapsnapshot and returns retainers for a specific node ID.

tools.name: close_heapsnapshot
tools.docstring: Closes a previously loaded memory heapsnapshot, freeing its memory.

tools.name: get_heapsnapshot_retaining_paths
tools.docstring: Loads a memory heapsnapshot and returns retaining paths for a specific node ID. This helps to understand why a node is not being garbage collected.

tools.name: get_heapsnapshot_edges
tools.docstring: Loads a memory heapsnapshot and returns outgoing edges (references) for a specific node ID.

tools.name: get_heapsnapshot_dominators
tools.docstring: Loads a memory heapsnapshot and returns the dominator chain for a specific node ID. This helps to identify which objects are keeping the target node alive.

tools.name: compare_heapsnapshots
tools.docstring: Loads two memory heapsnapshots and returns the comparison. If classIndex is provided, returns detailed diff for that class, otherwise returns summary diff.

tools.name: get_heapsnapshot_duplicate_strings
tools.docstring: Loads a memory heapsnapshot and returns duplicate strings grouped by their value.

tools.name: list_network_requests
tools.docstring: List all requests for the currently selected page since the last navigation.

tools.name: get_network_request
tools.docstring: Gets a network request by an optional reqid, if omitted returns the currently selected request in the DevTools Network panel.

tools.name: list_pages
tools.docstring: Get a list of pages${args?.categoryExtensions ? ' including extension service workers' : ''} open in the browser.

tools.name: select_page
tools.docstring: Select a page as a context for future tool calls.

tools.name: close_page
tools.docstring: Closes the page by its index. The last open page cannot be closed.

tools.name: new_page
tools.docstring: Open a new tab and load a URL. Use project URL if not specified otherwise.

tools.name: navigate_page
tools.docstring: Go to a URL, or back, forward, or reload. Use project URL if not specified otherwise.

tools.name: resize_page
tools.docstring: Resizes the selected page's window so that the page has specified dimension

tools.name: handle_dialog
tools.docstring: If a browser dialog was opened, use this command to handle it

tools.name: get_tab_id
tools.docstring: Get the tab ID of the page

tools.name: performance_start_trace
tools.docstring: Start a performance trace on the selected webpage. Use to find frontend performance issues, Core Web Vitals (LCP, INP, CLS), and improve page load speed.

tools.name: performance_stop_trace
tools.docstring: Stop the active performance trace recording on the selected webpage.

tools.name: performance_analyze_insight
tools.docstring: Provides more detailed information on a specific Performance Insight of an insight set that was highlighted in the results of a trace recording.

tools.name: screencast_start
tools.docstring: Starts recording a screencast (video) of the selected page in specified format.

tools.name: screencast_stop
tools.docstring: Stops the active screencast recording on the selected page.

tools.name: take_screenshot
tools.docstring: Take a screenshot of the page or element.

tools.name: evaluate_script
tools.docstring: Evaluate a JavaScript function inside the currently selected page${cliArgs?.categoryExtensions ? ' or service worker' : ''}. Returns the response as JSON, so returned values have to be JSON-serializable.

tools.name: take_snapshot
tools.docstring:
Take a text snapshot of the currently selected page based on the a11y tree. The snapshot lists page elements along with a unique
identifier (uid). Always use the latest snapshot. Prefer taking a snapshot over taking a screenshot. The snapshot indicates the element selected
in the DevTools Elements panel (if any).

tools.name: wait_for
tools.docstring: Wait for the specified text to appear on the selected page.

tools.name: list_3p_developer_tools
tools.docstring:
Lists all third-party developer tools the page exposes for providing runtime information.
Third-party developer tools can be called via the 'execute_3p_developer_tool()' MCP tool.
Alternatively, third-party developer tools can be executed by calling 'evaluate_script' and adding the
following command to the script:
\`window.__dtmcp.executeTool(toolName, params)\`
This might be helpful when the third-party developer tools return non-serializable values or when composing
third-party developer tools with additional functionality.

tools.name: execute_3p_developer_tool
tools.docstring: Executes a tool exposed by the page.

tools.name: list_webmcp_tools
tools.docstring: Lists all WebMCP tools the page exposes.

tools.name: execute_webmcp_tool
tools.docstring: Executes a WebMCP tool exposed by the page.

tools.name: screenshot
tools.docstring: Takes a screenshot

tools.name: navigate
tools.docstring: Loads a URL

tools.name: evaluate
tools.docstring: Evaluates a JavaScript script

tools.name: check-license
tools.docstring: Validate existence of license header

tools.name: enforce-zod-schema
tools.docstring: Disallow .nullable() and .object() in tool schemas. Use optional strings to represent complex objects.

tools.name: no-direct-third-party-imports
tools.docstring: Disallow value imports of bundled third-party packages outside of src/third_party/
